children.name: InsertIntoDataStore
children.purpose: Perform the actual insertion of the student record into the data store
children.behavior: Takes student_id and student_name, writes them as a new record to the students data store, and returns a boolean indicating success or failure of the write operation.

in_scope: Writing student_id and student_name to the data store

postconditions: If success, student record is persisted in data store
guarantees: No side effects beyond insertion attempt
children.stop_reason: Atomic write operation; further decomposition would require implementation details

decomposition_rationale: The parent's sole responsibility is to insert a student record. The only necessary child is the actual data store insertion function. No other children are needed because validation, duplicate checking, and error formatting are explicitly out of scope.
